73902.subject: Britain has crushed immigration, and harmed itself
73902.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4OTR9AAcAAAAAAiagrJxKCwgdRPsjkcDdVDFJJweAtxuruw3xw0_lAxfiqqutZ0YKsOXuz4IQlT5n2Jxob6AmGNcpd6GAzBdBDN9B09wHlkbaU_AQRax-VwC_ 

June 1st 2026

 

 
 
 

 The Economist Today
 


 Our best journalism, handpicked each day
 


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4OTR9AAcAAAAAAiagrJxLDUtaM06y4lSSTA20nVl22SB4JmniF1Cp6LdQTUzX4eSk69Cm9_v9y1MXeJode1qtpHvFtFBq921rm-H7Qk7LkokDuT0G-S_86XxC 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4OTR9AAcAAAAAAiagrJxMkUcthllZYlZ8n9mASa5HtJl_8yTbArGbwN_99nMfc5oUOf3XyeqyfcjNRwE30OJpFqYwlpEF372IFVf7ZFOfAxRVFZrl5VSWkcak 



 
 

Lane Greene

Senior digital editor



 Britain has stemmed the tide of immigration that landed on its shores after Brexit. 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4OTR9AAcAAAAAAiagrJxNRmWRyPI9YFiFQQqYPVD3Gp9eWYQ1PN037SFrInc01UuNXvx4bISa_8LFszowCIAXVh1XHukOAQ-P1Z1puegCRoXAMKL4ctI 
To its cost .
 Immigrants often make more than locals; employers want them; they pay extra taxes to fund the health service. Now that it’s far harder for workers to come, a greater share of immigrants will be asylum seekers—exactly the people xenophobes say they don’t want. 


 And Standard Chartered’s boss recently caused a fuss by describing replacing “lower-value human capital” with investment in AI. His sin was not callousness, but a tin

73901.subject: 📰 AI-curated Daily Crypto Digest - June 1
73901.body:
AI-Curated Daily Crypto Digest

Автор: Oleh Bezuhlyi


Автор: Oleh Bezuhlyi

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/ai-curated-daily-crypto-digest-june-1-oleh-bezuhlyi-el3bf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BayQe90SnTxiQPBIzyoRcFQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=2jp6Lnr9rHMIg1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mpveop9b~ip-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mpveop9b-ip
  
🧠 AI’s Crypto News Brief: Market sentiment remains cautious but structurally bullish as Bitcoin caps its best May performance since 2019. Despite regulatory headwinds from a White House veto, institutional momentum continues to accelerate via Ethereum ETF fee wars and stablecoin milestones. 🏛️Biden Vetoes Bipartisan Resolution to Overturn SEC’s SAB 121 President Joe Biden vetoed a measure that sought to repeal the SEC’s controversial Staff Accounting Bulletin 121, which mandates firms list customer crypto holdings as liabilities. Biden argued the "guardrails" are necessary for consumer protection, sparking criticism from industry leaders who claim it stifles bank-led crypto custody. Read full story 📉Uniswap Foundation Postpones High-Stakes Governance Vote The Uniswap Foundation delayed a critical vote on the "UNI fee switch" proposal, which would reward token holders for staking. The postponement followed concerns raised by an unnamed stakeholder, causing the UNI token to plunge over 8% as the community debates the influence of venture capital on decentralized governance. See details 🏛️ARK Invest Withdraws from Spot Ethereum ETF Partnership In a surprise move, Cathie Wood’s ARK Invest has pulled out of its joint bid for a spot Ethereum ETF with 21Shares. While ARK remains committed to its Bitcoin ETF, the filing was rebranded to the "21Shares Core Ethereum ETF," signaling a shift in strategy just as the industry enters the final S-1 approval phase. Read full story 📈Bitcoin Records Best May Performance Since 2019 Bitcoin closed the month of May with an approximate 11% gain, marking its strongest May performance in five years. The monthly rally was fueled by renewed spot ETF inflows and shifting macroeconomic sentiment, positioning the asset for a potential retest of its all-time high in June. See market analysis 💰Franklin Templeton Reveals Ultra-Low 0.19% Fee for Ethereum ETF Franklin Templeton has set a competitive tone for the upcoming spot Ethereum ETF market by disclosing a management fee of just 0.19%. This matches its aggressive pricing for the Bitcoin ETF (EZBC), intensifying the "fee war" among major issuers like BlackRock and Fidelity as they prepare for official trading. Read full story 🪙Ethena’s USDe Stablecoin Hits $3 Billion Supply Milestone Ethena Labs' synthetic dollar, USDe, reached a $3 billion supply just four

73898.subject: Data & Insights: Funding, Futures, DEX Volume Are All Down
73898.body:
The Data and Insights newsletter brings the highlights of the week from The Block’s Data Dashboard

( https://www.theblock.co/data/crypto-markets/spot )

Gm data enjoyers, happy June! This week, we’re watching markets digest Microstrategy selling the first of its Bitcoin (STRC holders rejoice!). Our team recently went live with our crypto funding dashboard ( https://www.theblock.co/data/alternative-crypto-metrics/crypto-funding ) , with over 13,000 startups, data going back to 2014, and every private VC deal in crypto tracked! We recommend giving it a check.

This week, we’re taking a look at futures trading volume and funding updates. We’ll also look at Polymarkets US stats, Hyperliquid’s rising share against CEXs, Binance’s foray into stocks, and the state of onchain trading. So grab a drink, take a seat, and let's dive in.

1: Future(s)-Proof

( https://www.theblock.co/data/crypto-markets/futures/total-futures-volumes )

Chart via The Block's Data Dashboard

*Key Insights* provided by The Block Research

* Total futures volume across major exchanges hit a 12-month low in May, closing the month at approximately $2.9 trillion, a level not seen since late 2023 and well below the $6-7 trillion monthly peaks recorded during last year's more active trading periods.

* The decline reflects a broader pullback in speculative activity across crypto markets, with spot volumes and on-chain activity similarly subdued heading into the summer.

* The composition of that volume remains heavily concentrated among a handful of exchanges, with Binance maintaining its dominant share, followed by OKX, Bybit, and Gate. Smaller venues have seen the most pronounced erosion in activity as traders consolidate onto deeper liquidity pools during quieter periods.
* Against this backdrop, the CFTC moved ( https://www.theblock.co/post/403040/cftc-opens-door-for-crypto-perpetual-future-contracts-in-us-as-coinbase-kalshi-move-forward ) to formally open the door for crypto perpetual futures contracts in the US, a structurally significant development for a derivatives product that has until now existed largely outside the reach of US regulation.
* Perpetual futures differ meaningfully from traditional futures instruments: they carry no expiry date, eliminating rollover costs and calendar risk. Funding rates, paid periodically between longs and shorts, continuously anchor the contract price to spot, functioning as a real-time gauge of market sentiment and leverage positioning. Combined with capital efficiency advantages over margin accounts, perps have become the dominant trading vehicle in crypto derivatives globally.

* The regulatory opening matters less for access, as a meaningful share of US-based traders already route through offshore exchanges via VPNs, and more for what legitimized domestic perp markets could unlock via institutional participation, cleaner compliance infrastructure, and onshore liquidity that does not require regulatory arbitrage.

73896.subject: One Block Weekly #13 | How Compound Changed Crypto Finance Forever
73896.body:
Hello,

Welcome back to One Block Weekly. One coin, one story.

Banks have been lending money and charging interest for centuries. In 2018, a small team decided to rebuild that system from scratch, on a blockchain, with no bank required.

🟦 Compound (COMP)

Robert Leshner had a background in economics and finance. In 2017 he asked a simple but radical question: what if crypto holders could earn interest on their assets without handing them to a bank?

The answer was Compound. Deposit crypto, earn interest automatically. Or borrow against holdings by putting up collateral. No application, no credit check, no human approval.

Interest rates adjust in real time based on supply and demand. The market sets the price.

Compound launched in 2018 and quietly became one of the most important building blocks in DeFi.

⚡ A Few Moments That Shaped Compound

June 2020 - Compound launched its COMP governance token and introduced liquidity mining. Users earned COMP simply for using the protocol. Within days it held more value than any other DeFi protocol. The DeFi summer had officially started.

2020 - At its peak Compound was holding over $10 billion in deposits. Institutions, funds, and retail users earning yield on crypto without touching a single bank.

September 2021 - A bug in a governance upgrade accidentally released $80 million in COMP tokens to users who were not entitled to them. Leshner publicly asked for the funds back, warning those who kept them would be reported to the IRS. The comment caused an uproar. Some returned the funds. Most did not.

2024 - Compound governance voted to send $24 million from its treasury to an external investment group. The community revolted, raising hard questions about the real risks of DAO decision-making.

🔢 What Makes It Different

Compound pioneered algorithmic interest rates on a blockchain. No negotiation, no paperwork, no relationship manager. Deposit an asset and start earning within seconds.

The COMP token launch also introduced liquidity mining to the world, a playbook dozens of DeFi protocols copied almost immediately and an entire era of crypto was born from it.

🤯 Three Weird Compound Facts

The IRS threat backfired. Leshner's public warning became one of the most mocked moments in DeFi history. The crypto community did not respond well to being threatened with tax authorities over a bug that was not their fault.

Compound helped invent yield farming. Moving funds between protocols to chase the highest returns was born largely from the COMP launch. A whole new category of crypto behaviour emerged from one governance decision.

It wrote the template for its rivals. Aave, one of Compound's biggest competitors, built directly on top of the same ideas. Compound essentially created the blueprint that DeFi lending still follows today.

Compound rarely makes headlines. No celebrity founder, no viral moments, no memecoin connections.

Just a protocol that showed lending

 Hi Oleg, 

 At Strava, we care deeply about developers, and the health of the developer 
ecosystem. There are now 241,000 Strava API developers, up from 185,000 last 
year. At its best, the Strava API allows users to utilize their favorite 
wearable, developers to build innovative businesses, and users to build 
personal tools against their own data.

 The tech landscape has shifted significantly in recent years, and Strava’s 
Developer Program must evolve along with it. AI companies are aggressively 
attempting to scrape platforms for training data, abuse APIs through 
intermediary layers, and provide zero-code AI tools that produce apps that 
hammer APIs. We've felt this firsthand – developer applications to our program 
are up 448% year-to-date, API intermediaries have violated security terms, and 
scraping attempts have degraded platform performance for everyone.

 To protect athletes, maintain platform health, and keep pace with how 
technology is evolving, we're making a set of changes to our API and Developer 
Program.

 * We're launching an official Strava MCP 
<https://press.strava.com/articles/strava-launches-mcp-connector?$web_only=true>
: A fl

73893.subject: Perps Come Onshore
73893.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/eaa70932-7ce4-467e-b338-a662ed575d6a/0xR_NL_Banner_Heading_Wordmark.jpg?t=1776775276)
Caption: 

Markets are becoming increasingly split. Equities continued grinding higher last week, while BTC lagged and crypto ETF outflows persisted. But under the surface, one part of the crypto market continues to stand out: perps. Hyperliquid-led markets remain one of the strongest areas in the space, supported by growing RWA volume, stronger mindshare among TradFi users, and now a fresh regulatory signal from the CFTC. This week, we look at the divergence between traditional markets and crypto, why perp-related assets keep outperforming, and what Kalshi’s newly approved BTCPERP actually changes for US market structure.

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/42e942ec-f31d-4957-8722-a3ac7b121e1e/0xR_NL_Market_Update.png?t=1767631151) [Market Update]
Caption: 

Last week saw a sharp divergence between traditional markets and crypto. The Nasdaq and S&P 500 continued their march higher, posting gains of 2.0% and 1.5%, respectively. Gold also finished the week in positive territory, up 0.76%. BTC, however, continued to struggle and ended the week down -3.8%.

The S&P 500 has now notched nine consecutive weeks of gains and is up 20% since the start of the US-Iran conflict. Continued [strong](https://www.reuters.com/business/wall-st-futures-steady-investors-await-mideast-deal-updates-dell-soars-2026-05-29/) earnings reports have kept investor enthusiasm around the AI trade firmly intact, pushing tech-heavy indices to fresh all-time highs. Despite lingering concerns around inflation stemming from the conflict, markets appear increasingly willing to look through near-term risks as [hopes](https://www.reuters.com/business/wall-st-futures-steady-investors-await-mideast-deal-updates-dell-soars-2026-05-29/) for a potential peace agreement continue to build.

Meanwhile, crypto ETFs have now recorded three consecutive weeks of outflows. BTC ETFs saw another -$1.3B of outflows last week, while ETH ETFs lost -$235M. Bitcoin DATs purchased roughly $2B worth of BTC in May, with Saylor remaining one of the largest sources of marginal demand. However, any slowdown in these purchases alongside continued ETF outflows could help explain BTC's recent underperformance relative to both equities and gold.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/552e66db-dc7e-47d0-90ae-60175d48809a/image.png?t=1780310839)
Caption: 

Despite BTC's weakness, select areas of the market continue to show impressive strength and an increasingly decoupled performance from the broader crypto landscape. Perps remained the best performing sector, gaining 16.3% on the w
